title: 合并模块开启后，右上角状态栏无法点击。

title: Base M5: CPU frequency never updates — `voltage-states22-sram` does not exist on base M5, so FrequencyReader.read() always returns early
author: Abrar118
body: On a base M5, the CPU frequency values are static and never update. "Super cores" frequency is blank; "Efficiency cores" / "All cores" show stale values that never change. Follow-up to #3325 (closed as "covered in #3414"). #3414 is an M5 **Pro** menu-bar *color* bug; its fix (`68b7e5f`) does not touch the frequency reader, so the base-M5 frequency bug is still present in v3.0.8. **Details** - Device: MacBook Pro, base **Apple M5** (4 Super + 6 Efficiency, 2 clusters) - macOS: 26.5.2 (25F84) - Stats: 3.0.8 (825) --- ### Root cause (probable) `SystemKit.getFrequencies(for:)` (`Kit/plugins/SystemKit.swift:510-528`) reads the performance-cluster DVFS table from `voltage-states22-sram` on all M5-gen chips: ```swift if isM5GenOrNewer { if let data = props.value(forKey: "voltage-states1-sram") { eFreq = ... } if let data = props.value(forKey: "voltage-states22-sram") { pFreq = ... } if let data = props.value(forKey: "voltage-states5-sram") { sFreq = ... } } ``` **`voltage-states22-sram` does not exist on the base M5** — that key appears to be M5 Pro/Max only. So `pFreq == []`. `FrequencyReader.read()` (`Modules/CPU/readers.swift:357`) then bails out on every tick: ```swift guard !self.isReading, (!self.eCoreFreqs.isEmpty || !self.sCoreFreqs.isEmpty) && !self.pCoreFreqs.isEmpty, ... else { return } ``` `!self.pCoreFreqs.isEmpty` is `false` → **no frequency is ever emitted at all** (not E, not S, not the aggregate). This matches the symptom exactly: values frozen rather than wrong. Introduced by `5432cc2` (#3250), which broadened `isM5ProMaxOrNewer` → `isM5GenOrNewer` and thereby routed the base M5 through the Pro/Max `voltage-states22-sram` path. Secondary (currently masked by the guard): since `sCoreCount != 0` on M5-gen, `readers.swift:375-385` looks for an `MCPU` IOReport channel for p-cores. The base M5 has no M cluster, so that would stay empty regardless. --- ### System dumps (base M5) `ioreg -p IODeviceTree -n pmgr -r` — **no `voltage-states22-sram`**: ``` "voltage-states0" "voltage-states1" "voltage-states1-sram" "voltage-states2" "voltage-states5" "voltage-states5-sram" "voltage-states8" "voltage-states9" "voltage-states9-sram" "voltage-states11" ``` `ioreg -p IODeviceTree -n cpus -r -d 1` — **no `m-core-count`**: ``` "cpu-cluster-count" = <02000000> // 2 "p-core-count" = <04000000> // 4 "e-core-count" = <06000000> // 6 ``` `ioreg -p IOService -n AppleARMPE -r -l | grep cluster-type` — normal `E`/`P`, not the `M`/`P` seen on M5 Pro in #3414: ``` "cluster-type" = <"E"> x6 "cluster-type" = <"P"> x4 ``` `sysctl`: ``` hw.nperflevels: 2 hw.perflevel0.name: Super hw.perflevel0.physicalcpu: 4 hw.perflevel1.name: Efficiency hw.perflevel1.physicalcpu: 6 ``` --- ### Suggested fix On M5-gen, treat the M (performance) cluster as optional: 1. In `getFrequencies`, fall back when `voltage-states22-sram` is absent — base M5 is E + S only, so leave `pFreq` empty rather than assuming it exists. 2. Relax the `read()` guard so it doesn't require `pCoreFreqs` to be non-empty; gate each cluster independently. Happy to test a build — I have the affected hardware. # Preview

title: CPU power consumption always shows 0 W on M1 MacBook Air running macOS 27 Beta 2
author: ZENdgfdfd
body: Describe the bug After updating Stats to version 3.0.8, the CPU power consumption value in the Sensors/Power section always remains at 0 W. The GPU power consumption is displayed normally and changes according to GPU activity, but the CPU power value stays at 0 W regardless of CPU activity. Steps to reproduce Open Stats. Open the Sensors module. Check the CPU and GPU power consumption values. Run a CPU-intensive task. Observe that the CPU power value remains at 0 W, while the GPU power value is displayed normally. Expected behavior The CPU power consumption value should display the current CPU power usage and change according to CPU load. Actual behavior The CPU power consumption value always displays 0 W. Environment Device: MacBook Air (M1, 2020) Processor: Apple M1 macOS: macOS 27 Beta 3 Stats version: 3.0.8 GPU power reading: Working normally CPU power reading: Always 0 W Additional information This may be related to changes in macOS 27 Beta 3 , as the operating system is currently a beta release. I can provide screenshots or additional diagnostic information if needed.

title: Occasionally Crash (SIGABRT) in the Disk preview when a disk is mounted/unmounted
author: LH-and-FPGA
body:
**Describe the bug**
Sometimes, when I unplug an external drive and then open the disk module, the module’s preview crashes the entire app if the set of mounted disks changes while the preview is visible. The logs show a `SIGABRT`. This has happened to me several times a day.

After reading the code, I suspect that this crash comes from `Preview.capacityCallback` in `Modules/Disk/preview.swift`, which updates the "All disks" `NSGridView` with incremental row surgery. On removal it detaches a row's cell views (`removeFromSuperview()`) before calling `removeRow(at:)`, and it also removes merged separator rows; that leaves the grid's internal state inconsistent and `-[NSGridView removeRowAtIndex:]` aborts with an internal assertion.

**Details:**
 - Device: MBP M5 Pro
 - macOS: 26.5.2
 - Application version: 3.0.7

**Additional context**
I've traced the root cause (maybe) and have a fix ready on my fork. Happy to open a PR referencing this issue if you're OK with the approach. Thank you!

title: Internal SSD shows 0% in Menubar on Mac mini M4 (macOS 26.5.2)
author: larrytang520
body: **Describe the bug** The internal SSD disk usage widget in the menu bar displays 0% instead of the actual storage usage. The disk section appears enabled in settings, but the menu bar value never updates from 0. **Environment** - Mac model: Mac mini M4 (16GB RAM / 256GB SSD) - macOS version: 26.5.2 - Application version: 3.0.7 - Disk type: Internal SSD (Macintosh HD, APFS) **To Reproduce** 1. Open Stats settings 2. Enable Disk module in the menu bar 3. Observe the menu bar disk widget **Expected behavior** The menu bar should display the actual used/free percentage of the internal SSD, consistent with what Disk Utility or "About This Mac" reports. **Actual behavior** The disk widget consistently shows 0%, regardless of actual disk usage. **Additional context** - Disk section is confirmed enabled in Stats settings. - ATA SMART reader toggle has been checked (both enabled and disabled states tested) — issue persists in both cases. - Disk Utility reports correct capacity and usage independently of Stats. **Screenshots**

title: Problems that occur during module merging
author: Flowershangfromthebranches
body: **Describe the bug** I'm not sure if it's an issue with macOS 27. Before the upgrade, after enabling merge mode, clicking any module in the merged modules would show the details of the module clicked. But now, after updating to 27, no matter which module I click, it always only shows the details of the module in the center. For example, if the sensor module is in the center, no matter whether I click on the CPU, GPU, RAM, or the disk and network speed modules, it only shows the details of the sensor module. **Details:** - Device: [MacBook air m3] - macOS: [macos 27 beta 3] - Application version: [3.0.7]

title: [Bug] Top Processes reports ~2 GB memory for Clash Verge while total RSS is only ~130 MB
author: Starfie1d1272
body:
### Description

The RAM module's "Top Processes" section reports about 2.0 GB of memory usage for Clash Verge, while the total RSS of all related Clash Verge / Mihomo processes is only about 130 MB.

This makes the process list appear misleading and suggests a large memory leak that does not seem to exist.

### Stats output

Stats reports:

- Clash Verge: ~2.08 GB
- Memory pressure: Normal

### Actual processes

```bash
ps -axo pid,ppid,rss,command | grep -Ei 'clash|mihomo|clash verge' | grep -v grep
```

Output:

```text
514     1   5824  ...clash-verge-service
81537   1  43424  .../Clash Verge.app/.../clash-verge
81595 514  82256  .../verge-mihomo
```

Total RSS:

```bash
ps -axo rss=,command= | awk '
tolower($0) ~ /clash|mihomo/ {sum+=$1}
END {printf "Total: %.2f GB\n", sum/1048576}'
```

Output:

```text
Total: 0.13 GB
```

At the same time, `memory_pressure` reports:

```text
System-wide memory free percentage: 46%
Pages throttled: 0
```

### Expected behavior

The "Top Processes" memory value should either:

1. approximately match the actual memory attributed to the application's processes, or
2. clearly indicate which memory metric is being displayed if it is not RSS.

### Actual behavior

Stats reports approximately 2.08 GB for Clash Verge, around 15x the summed RSS of all matching processes.

### Environment

- macOS: 26.5.2
- Stats: 3.0.5
- Device: MacBook Air
- Architecture: Apple Silicon (arm64)
- Clash Verge Rev: 2.5.1

title: Network widget keeps empty pictogram spacing when pictogram is disabled
author: batuozdemir
body:
When using the Network widget in the macOS menu bar, disabling the pictogram/icon option hides the blue/red network indicator dots visually, but the widget still reserves horizontal space for them.

As a result, the Network widget takes up more menu bar space than expected (my menu bar is kind of crowded), even though the pictograms are turned off.

**Expected behavior:**

When the pictogram is disabled, the widget should remove both:

-the pictogram itself
-the horizontal space reserved for the pictogram

The widget should become more compact and only show the enabled text/values.

Thanks!

title: Feature Request: Calendar with consolidated widget or variable icon
author: macmeister
body: I'm loving Stats. Thank you! I only recently noticed a tweak to conserve space in the menu bar when I went to replace Dato on one of my Macs. l still use it for the Calendar and items list it provides. This is more a quality of life menu enhancement, other than a necessity. I've attached 3 menu bar clocks to see if anyone would be willing to consider tackling this handy icon improvement. Of the 3 examples, the top center one is preferred and reduces the most space. I've also attached the settings surrounding the options for the Dato GUI as an example, which applies very little, but is there as an FYI for a possible Icon dropdown.

title: "Average System Total" power shown instead of "System Total"
author: HuweyII
body:
[**Describe](url) the bug**
When the "Sensors" widget is enabled and all modules disabled except "System Total" the value displayed on the bar is the "Average System Total" instead of the "System Total".  In this scenario only the widget showing text is used (i.e. "15W", not a graph or chart)

i.e. Clicking on the sensor widget shows System Total = 10W while Average System Total = 15W.    15W is what is shown on the bar, despite System Total being the only module selected.

**Details:**
 - Device: Macbook Pro 16" M5 Max
 - macOS: 26.5.2
 - Application version: 3.0.7
